threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep bringing an umbrella out, but the locals just wear raincoats and act like it’s no big deal. That’s how you know you’re new.
Last night on the 133, a couple argued over who forgot the snacks. Meanwhile, the bus driver just shook his head, clearly seen it all before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was rooted here when I spotted the same old man watering the same patch of weeds outside my window every Sunday, like clockwork.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner, then stumble into a techno club at 3 AM with the smell of currywurst still on your clothes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how quickly they pull out an umbrella when the fog rolls in. Tourists just stand there, confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading commuters with a ukulele on the 21 today. He had a whole crowd just waiting for the next stop. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all quirky little shops, but they often miss the quiet, cobbled streets where locals just sit and sip coffee, away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their confusion at the 'no entry' signs on streets like Consell de Cent—seriously, it's designed to keep traffic out, not them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest Tube, like there’s a chance someone will actually direct you to one.
The 196 bus last night had a guy reciting Shakespeare loudly; the driver just shrugged and kept driving, like it was a normal Tuesday.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sipping coffee on Karl-Marx-Straße, I heard a toddler arguing with a pigeon over a pretzel. That's when I felt the real heartbeat of Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a café with a library for dogs—complete with dog-friendly books and treats. It’s a bark-tastic hangout!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they can name the taco truck that shows up after the first fog rolls in—it's like a ritual, not just a late-night snack.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a Muni driver chatting with a street musician at Civic Center, both laughing while the bus idled. Just another day in SF’s vibrant chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just bars and trendy shops, but they miss the quiet corners where locals squeeze fresh orange juice every morning.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them asking for a ‘tapa’ on every menu instead of just a drink. It’s the little things that give them away.
